title: [FIRRTL] Abstract Reset Errors Print Whole Module
body:
If a circuit has an uninformed reset, this is currently printing the whole module body. This should just print the module definition (or the port once source locators are hooked up to those). This should be a simple change from `module.emitOpError() << ...` to `mlir::emitError(module.getLoc()) << ...`

Consider:

```
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input a: Reset
```

This produces:

```
# firtool Tmp.fir
Tmp.fir:2:10: error: 'firrtl.module' op has an abstract reset type port "a" after InferResets
  module Foo:
         ^
Tmp.fir:2:10: note: see current operation: 
"firrtl.module"() ({
^bb0(%arg0: !firrtl.reset):
}) {annotations = [], portAnnotations = [], portDirections = false, portNames = ["a"], portSyms = [], portTypes = [!firrtl.reset], sym_name = "Foo"} : () -> ()
```
